chips.text: no acute visual loss reported if applicable
menstrual or sexual function context reviewed if clinically appropriate
multi-specialty referral documented if clinician arranged
hand and foot size documented if relevant
visual field testing documented if assessed
thirst and urine output reviewed if relevant
bone and joint pain reviewed if relevant

speed_preset.text: no acute visual loss reported if applicable

history_draft.text: Patient presents with pituitary symptoms. [Headache]. [Visual symptoms]. [Menstrual/sexual function]. [Growth/weight changes]. [Fatigue/weakness]. [Galactorrhoea].

exam_prompts.text: Documented if assessed or measured. Blood pressure documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Visual fields documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Neurological screen documented.

investigation_options.text: Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Pituitary MRI reviewed.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Hormonal profile reviewed.

clinical_blockers: The broad symptoms workflow does not encode incidentaloma, adult, lesion-size, pregnancy, endocrine, or surgical qualifiers.

Seizure, neurological screen, general appearance, growth or weight change, hand or foot size, thirst or urine output, bone or joint pain, previous records, hormone replacement, generic clinician plan, follow-up, fixed intervals, PRN wording, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, hormone-result interpretation, hormone replacement, treatment, surgery, referral urgency, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
